mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts indicates sectoral rebalancing toward capital-intensive growth, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5 percent while unemployment remains below 4.5 percent, the economy operates at peak capacity with tight labor markets, supporting sustained equity premium expansion and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when both conditions align, or the joint signal produces a negative information coefficient across all out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands aggregate demand for corporate goods and services, directly lifting earnings per share and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no correlation with GDP acceleration, or the signal yields a negative rank IC over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent annualized forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral following inflation spikes above the threshold, or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, which reinforces corporate revenue guidance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns during confirmed spending surges, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in a rolling regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production surges past 103 while housing starts remain stagnant, supply chain bottlenecks and inventory gluts emerge, squeezing profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive despite the production-housing divergence, or the mechanism fails to predict equity drawdowns in out-of-sample testing.
